title: veronica spicata subsp. glandulifera
intro: Veronica spicata subsp. glandulifera is a subspecies of the flowering plant Veronica spicata, commonly known as the spikes speedwell, which is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, prized for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Veronica spicata subsp. glandulifera include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
